volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Ghost (Section 4.3.925 - Section 4.4.978)

sections.label: Section 4.3.925
sections.original: Quid ? tibi umquam quicquam , postquam tuos sum , verborum dedi ?
sections.translation: Why , have I ever imposed upon you in anything , since I was your servant ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.926
sections.original: Ego enim cavi recte : eam mihi des gratiam atque animo meo .
sections.translation: But I ’ ve taken good care of that ; I owe thanks to myself and my own judgment for that . If I ’ m only on my guard against you solely , I ’ m quite wise enough .

sections.label: Section 4.3.927b
sections.translation: I agree with you .

sections.label: Section 4.3.928
sections.original: Nunc abi rús , dic me advenisse filio .
sections.translation: Now be off into the country ; tell my son that I ’ ve arrived .

sections.label: Section 4.3.928b
sections.translation: I ’ ll do as you wish .

sections.label: Section 4.3.929_930
sections.original: Curriculo iube in urbem veniat iam simul tecum .
sections.translation: Run with all speed ; bid him come to the city at once together with you .

sections.label: Section 4.3.929_930b
sections.translation: Very well . ( Aside .) Now I ’ ll betake myself this way by the back-door to my boon-companions ; I ’ ll tell them that things are quiet here , and how I have kept him away from here .

sections.label: Section 4.4.933
sections.original: Hic quidem neque convivarum sonitus , item ut antehac fuit ,
sections.translation: Really , I don ’ t hear either the sound of revellers here , as once it was , nor yet the music-girl singing , nor any one else .

sections.label: Section 4.4.935
sections.original: Quae illaec res est ? quid illisce homines quaerunt apud aedis meas ?
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter here ? What are these people seeking at my house ? What do they want ? What are they peeping in for ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.936b
sections.translation: I shall proceed to knock at the door . ( Knocks .) Hallo there , unlock the door ! Hallo , Tranio ! are you going to open it , I say ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.937b
sections.translation: What story ’ s this , I wonder ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.938
sections.original: Etiamne aperis ? Callidamati nostro adversum venimus .
sections.translation: Are you going to open it , I say ? I ’ ve come to fetch my master Callidamates .

sections.label: Section 4.4.939
sections.original: Heus vos , pueri , quid istic agitis ? quid istas aedis frangitis ?
sections.translation: Harkye , you lads ! what are you doing there ? Why are you breaking down that door ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.946
sections.translation: Our master ’ s at a drinking-party here .

sections.label: Section 4.4.946b
sections.translation: Your master at a drinking-party here ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.946c
sections.translation: I say so .

sections.label: Section 4.4.947
sections.translation: You ’ re carrying the joke too far , my lad .

sections.label: Section 4.4.947b
sections.translation: We ’ ve come to fetch him .

sections.label: Section 4.4.948
sections.translation: What person ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.948b
sections.original: Ero nostro . quaeso , quotiens dicendumst tibi ?
sections.translation: Our master . Prithee , how often must I tell you ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.949
sections.original: Puere , nemo hic habitat . nam te esse arbitror puerum probum .
sections.translation: There ’ s no one living here my lad ; for I do think that you are a decent lad .

sections.label: Section 4.4.950
sections.original: Non hic Philolaches adulescens habitat hisce in aedibus ?
sections.translation: Doesn ’ t a young gentleman called Philolaches live in this house ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.952
sections.translation: This old fellow ’ s crack-brained , surely .

sections.label: Section 4.4.952b
sections.translation: You are entirely mistaken , respected sir [*] ; for unless he moved from here to-day or yesterday , I know for certain that he ’ s living here .

sections.label: Section 4.4.954b
sections.original: Quin sex menses iam hic nemo habitat .
sections.translation: Why , no one has been living here for these six months past .

sections.label: Section 4.4.954c
sections.translation: You are dreaming .

sections.label: Section 4.4.955
sections.translation: What , I ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.955b
sections.translation: You .

sections.label: Section 4.4.955c
sections.original: Tu ne . sine me cum puero loqui .
sections.translation: Don ’ t you be impertinent . Let me speak to the lad . ( Pointing to PHANISCUS .)

sections.label: Section 4.4.956
sections.translation: No one lives there ? O dear —

sections.label: Section 4.4.956b
sections.original: Habitat profecto , nám heri et nudius tertius ,
sections.translation: It ’ s the fact .

sections.label: Section 4.4.957
sections.original: quartus , quintus , sextus , usque postquam hinc peregre eius pater
sections.translation: Really ! why , yesterday and the day before , [*] four , five , six days ago , all along , in fact , since his father went abroad from here , eating and drinking have never ceased for a single three days here .

sections.label: Section 4.4.959
sections.translation: What is it you say ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.959b
sections.original: Triduom unum est haud intermissum hic esse et bibi ,
sections.translation: That eating and drinking have never stopped for a single three days here ,

sections.label: Section 4.4.960
sections.translation: bringing in wenches , living like Greeks , hiring harpists and music-girls .

sections.label: Section 4.4.961b
sections.translation: Who was it did this ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.961c
sections.translation: Philolaches .

sections.label: Section 4.4.961d
sections.translation: What Philolaches ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.962
sections.translation: He whose father I take to be Theuropides .

sections.label: Section 4.4.962b
sections.translation: O dear , O dear ! I ’ m utterly undone , if he says the truth in this . I ’ ll continue to question him still Do you say that this Philolaches , whoever he is , has been in the habit of drinking here together

sections.label: Section 4.4.965
sections.translation: with your master ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.965b
sections.translation: Here , I tell you .

sections.label: Section 4.4.965c
sections.translation: My lad , contrary to your appearance , you are a fool . See now , please , that you ’ ve not perchance been dropping in somewhere for an afternoon ’ s whet [*] , and have been drinking there a little more than was enough .

sections.label: Section 4.4.967b
sections.translation: What do you mean ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.968
sections.original: Ita dico , ne ad alias aedis perperam deveneris .
sections.translation: Just what I say : don ’ t be going by mistake to other persons ’ houses .

sections.label: Section 4.4.969
sections.original: Scio qua me ire oportet et quo venerim novi locum .
sections.translation: I know where I ought to go , and the place to which I was to come .

sections.label: Section 4.4.970
sections.original: Philolaches hic habitat , cuius est pater Theopropides .
sections.translation: Philolaches lives here , whose father is Theuropides ; and who , after his father went away to trade , made free a music-girl here .

sections.label: Section 4.4.972b
sections.translation: Philolaches , say you ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.972c
sections.translation: Just so ; Philematium , I mean .

sections.label: Section 4.4.973
sections.translation: For how much ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.973b
sections.translation: For thirty talents .

sections.label: Section 4.4.973d
sections.translation: No , by Apollo [*] ; you mean minae .

sections.label: Section 4.4.974
sections.original: Ain minis triginta amicam destinatam Philolachi ?
sections.translation: Do you say that a mistress was purchased for Philolaches for thirty minae ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.975
sections.translation: I do say so .

sections.label: Section 4.4.975b
sections.translation: And that he gave her her freedom ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.975c
sections.translation: I do say so .

sections.label: Section 4.4.975d
sections.translation: And that after his father had departed hence abroad , he has been carousing here continually with your master ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.977b
sections.translation: I do say so .

sections.label: Section 4.4.977c
sections.original: Quid ? is aedis emit has hinc proxumas ?
sections.translation: Well , has he made purchase of the house next door here ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.978
sections.translation: I don ’ t say so .
